models.name: Qwen 2.5 7B AWQ
models.description: Fast 7B instruction-tuned model with AWQ-Marlin quantization. Good for simple chat and lookups.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 14B AWQ
models.description: Mid-size instruction-tuned model. Good balance of quality and speed for coding and analysis.

models.name: Qwen3.5 27B AWQ
models.description: Dense 27B model with native thinking mode. Dual-GPU tensor parallel. enforce-eager for WSL stability.

models.name: Qwen3 32B AWQ
models.description: Primary reasoning model with thinking mode and tool calling. Dual-GPU TP=2, 40K context (max_position_embeddings limit).

models.name: Qwen3.5 27B Abliterated
models.description: Qwen3.5-27B abliterated (refusal direction removed). Hybrid SSM-transformer. BNB NF4 4-bit on single GPU. TP=2 produces NaN due to BNB quant_state sharding bug in vLLM — TP=1 only.

models.name: Qwen3.5 27B Abliterated AWQ
models.description: Qwen3.5-27B abliterated (refusal direction removed). AWQ-Marlin 4-bit, dual-GPU TP=2. -v2 checkpoint: merged in_proj_qkvz (fused, AWQ) + dequantized out_proj.weight (BF16) to match vLLM Qwen3.5 hybrid model expectations. max-num-batched-tokens 4096 prevents SSM profiling OOM.

models.name: Qwopus 27B BNB (Opus Distilled)
models.description: Claude Opus 4.6 reasoning distilled into Qwen3.5-27B. BNB 4-bit, dual-GPU TP=2 test. AWQ quantization pending qwen3_5 transformers support.

models.name: Qwopus3.5 27B v3 (Opus Distilled, GGUF)
models.description: Qwopus3.5-27B v3 — Claude Opus 4.6 reasoning distilled into Qwen3.5-27B with SFT+LoRA. Process-level reasoning, strong tool calling (97.56% HumanEval), multimodal vision via mmproj. Q8_0 near-lossless GGUF via llama.cpp, dual-GPU. 256K native context.

models.name: Huihui Qwopus3.5 27B v3 Abliterated PolarQuant Q5
models.description: Huihui Qwopus3.5-27B-v3 (Opus distilled) abliterated, quantized by caiovicentino1 with PolarQuant INT4 symmetric (group_size=128, CompressedTensors format). ~14GB VRAM, fits single RTX 3090. 168 tok/s on A100 with Marlin kernel. cos_sim > 0.998 vs BF16. vLLM only (not llama.cpp). 48-layer dense Qwen3.5 architecture.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 32B AWQ
models.description: Large instruction-tuned model. Strong at complex reasoning and long context tasks.

models.name: Llama 3 8B
models.description: Meta's Llama 3 8B instruction-tuned. Full precision, general purpose.

models.name: DeepSeek Coder 7B
models.description: Specialized code generation model. Strong at code completion and understanding.

models.name: FLUX.1 Schnell (legacy)
models.description: FLUX.1 Schnell — previous generation fast image model. Superseded by FLUX.2-dev. Kept as fallback.

models.name: FLUX.2 Dev
models.description: FLUX.2-dev (Nov 2025) — current SOTA open image generation. Multi-reference (up to 10 images), 60% typography accuracy, up to 4MP output. Needs 16GB VRAM at FP8 (40% faster than FP16). Served by image-server on gaming PC (RTX 3090). This is the active model in docker-compose.gaming.yml.

models.name: Stable Diffusion 3.5 Large
models.description: SD 3.5 Large at FP8 with TensorRT: 11GB VRAM (40% reduction from 18GB FP16). Three text encoders, MMDiT architecture, excellent prompt adherence. Competitive with FLUX.2 for photorealism. Served by image-server — swap MODEL_ID to activate.

models.name: Stable Diffusion 3.5 Medium
models.description: SD 3.5 Medium — 6GB VRAM. Fits 3070 or 3090. Good quality at reduced compute. Best option when gaming PC is occupied or for lower-priority generation tasks.

models.name: LTX-Video
models.description: LTX-Video by Lightricks. Best open-source video model for consumer hardware. 30fps at 1216x704, real-time generation speed. Int8 mode: 6GB VRAM (fits 3070). Full precision: 12GB. Text-to-video and image-to-video. ComfyUI integration. Requires video-server container (not yet deployed — see task).

models.name: CogVideoX 5B
models.description: CogVideoX-5B (Zhipu/Tsinghua). Text-to-video, 6-second clips at 720x480. Fits single RTX 3090. Strong Diffusers support, good image-to-video quality. Requires video-server container (not yet deployed — see task).

models.name: HunyuanVideo (540p FP8)
models.description: Tencent HunyuanVideo — 13B transformer video model, SOTA open-source video gen. 540p FP8 ~40GB fits dual RTX 3090. Multi-GPU via xDiT Unified Sequence Parallelism. Supports text-to-video and image-to-video. 720p FP8 is ~50GB (too tight for 48GB). Requires video-server with xDiT support.

models.name: HunyuanVideo 1.5
models.description: HunyuanVideo 1.5 — improved quality over v1.0. FP16 at 14-15GB fits single RTX 3090. Supports 720p to 1080p generation. Significantly better motion quality than base. Requires video-server container.

models.name: Wan 2.2 T2V-14B
models.description: Wan 2.2 Text-to-Video 14B — strong open-source T2V at 720p. ~24GB BF16 for dual-GPU FSDP+xDiT. Strong motion dynamics and prompt adherence. Also supports image-to-video. Requires video-server with FSDP support.

models.name: HunyuanImage 3.0 NF4
models.description: Tencent HunyuanImage 3.0 — 80B MoE image generation model. NF4 community quant by EricRollei (~48GB). Marginal fit on dual RTX 3090 (48GB total). SOTA image quality, text rendering, photorealism. Chinese → English instruction support. Requires diffusers image-server; NF4 loading via BitsAndBytes.

models.name: Chatterbox Turbo TTS
models.description: Chatterbox Turbo (350M) by Resemble AI. Paralinguistic tags ([cough], [laugh]), zero-shot voice cloning, Perth watermarking. v0.1.2 (June 2025) — current deployed TTS. Fast 1-step inference.

models.name: Chatterbox Multilingual TTS
models.description: Chatterbox Multilingual (500M) — 23+ languages, zero-shot voice cloning. Same Resemble AI v0.1.2 release. Swap model variant in tts-server to activate. Not yet deployed alongside Turbo.

models.name: Kokoro 82M TTS
models.description: Kokoro-82M — fastest open TTS. Sub-300ms latency, <1GB VRAM. Quality comparable to much larger models. No voice cloning. Ideal always-on TTS for agent responses and real-time use. Requires tts-server model-switching support (see task).

models.name: F5-TTS
models.description: F5-TTS by OpenVoiceAI. Diffusion-based, zero-shot voice cloning, 33x real-time with fast variant. Better expressiveness than Chatterbox Turbo. MIT license. Requires tts-server model-switching support (see task).

models.name: Qwen3 TTS
models.description: Qwen3-TTS (Jan 2026). Best speaker similarity (0.789) — outperforms ElevenLabs Multilingual v2. 3-second rapid voice cloning, streaming support. 7-9GB VRAM. FlashAttention 2 gives 30-40% speedup. Note: slight Chinese accent artifacts on some English voices. Requires tts-server model-switching support.

models.name: Orpheus TTS 3B
models.description: Orpheus TTS 3B (late 2025). New category: emotional speech generation that rivals premium cloud services. 6-8GB VRAM. Human-like expressiveness with natural prosody and emotion. Requires tts-server model-switching support.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 0.5B
models.description: Qwen2.5 0.5B instruction-tuned. Ultra-lightweight, ~1GB VRAM. Always-on candidate for simple lookups, classification, and quick tool calls. Native BF16.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 1.5B AWQ
models.description: Qwen2.5 1.5B AWQ-Marlin quantized. ~2GB VRAM — good always-on candidate for the 3070. Strong tool calling at minimal resource cost.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 3B AWQ
models.description: Qwen2.5 3B AWQ-Marlin. Sweet spot for the RTX 3070 — noticeably smarter than 1.5B at only 3GB VRAM. Solid tool calling, good for coding and analysis.

models.name: Phi-4-mini 3.8B
models.description: Microsoft Phi-4-mini 3.8B. Best-in-class instruction following at small size. BNB 4-bit for 3070 compatibility (~3GB). Tool call parser needs verification on vLLM v0.17.1 — add --tool-call-parser once confirmed. Requires vLLM >= 0.7.3.

models.name: Gemma 3 2B
models.description: Google Gemma 3 2B instruction-tuned. Native BF16, 4GB VRAM. Well-rounded capability — good for dialogue, coding, and prompt-based tool calling. No dedicated vLLM tool parser; uses auto-tool-choice with prompt format.

models.name: Gemma 3 1B
models.description: Google Gemma 3 1B instruction-tuned. Native BF16, ~2GB VRAM. Ultra-light — suitable for simple tasks, on-device-style workloads, or as a fast fallback. Also available as QAT GGUF for mobile deployment.

models.name: Gemma 4 E2B
models.description: Google Gemma 4 E2B — 5.1B total, 2.3B active params. Dense multimodal (text+image+audio). 128K context. Smallest Gemma 4, good for lightweight tasks.

models.name: Gemma 4 E4B
models.description: Google Gemma 4 E4B — 8B total, 4.5B active params. Dense multimodal (text+image+audio). 128K context. Strong mid-size model, fits single RTX 3090 at BF16.

models.name: Gemma 4 26B-A4B (MoE)
models.description: Google Gemma 4 26B-A4B — 26B total, only 4B active params (MoE). Near-31B quality at fraction of compute. BNB 4-bit ~14GB fits single RTX 3090. 256K context. Multimodal (text+image). Best efficiency-to-quality ratio in the Gemma 4 family.

models.name: Gemma 4 31B
models.description: Google Gemma 4 31B — strongest in the family. Dense 31B, BNB 4-bit ~17GB fits single RTX 3090. 256K context. Multimodal (text+image). MMLU Pro 85.2%, AIME 2026 89.2%, LiveCodeBench 80%. No AWQ yet — switch when available.

models.name: SmolLM2 1.7B
models.description: HuggingFace SmolLM2 1.7B. Trained on 11T tokens (FineMath + Stack-Edu). Designed for on-device use — also fine-tunable with minimal data. Available in GGUF/ONNX/MLC for mobile deployment. Exports cleanly to GGUF for Godot (GDLlama).

models.name: SmolLM2 360M
models.description: HuggingFace SmolLM2 360M. ~680MB native, ~450MB at Q6 GGUF. Designed for on-device/edge. Fine-tunable for specific game dialogue or task schemas. Primary use case is GGUF export for GDLlama (Godot) or MLC-LLM (mobile). vLLM entry allows prefetch to huggingface-cache.

models.name: SmolLM2 135M
models.description: HuggingFace SmolLM2 135M. ~260MB native, ~160MB at Q6 GGUF. Smallest usable instruction-following model. Suitable for Godot NPC dialogue with domain fine-tuning, or mobile app bundle where size is critical.

models.name: Gemma 3 270M (FunctionGemma)
models.description: Google Gemma 3 270M — FunctionGemma variant specifically optimized for on-device tool/function calling (released Jan 2026). ~320MB at Q4 GGUF. Ideal for game agents (Godot via GDLlama) that need to call game APIs. Fine-tune on your tool schema for tight mobile bundling. Verify exact HF ID — may be google/functiongemma-270m.

models.name: GLM-4.7-Flash 30B MoE
models.description: GLM-4.7-Flash: 30B total / 3B active MoE. Fast inference, strong tool calling, interleaved thinking. Recommended by Unsloth for Claude Code agentic tasks. BNB 4-bit for single-GPU. Avoid AWQ variants — community AWQs calibrated without tool calling data.

models.name: GLM-Z1-9B
models.description: GLM-Z1-9B: 9B dense reasoning model with thinking mode. Native BF16 — fits single RTX 3090 (18GB). Strong at math and coding. No AWQ available; native precision preferred.

models.name: GLM-Z1-32B
models.description: GLM-Z1-32B: 32B dense model with deep thinking. Strong coding and analysis. BNB 4-bit for single-GPU (~17GB). No community AWQ available.

models.name: GLM-Z1-Rumination-32B
models.description: GLM-Z1-Rumination-32B: 32B dense with extended deep thinking (rumination). Designed for long research tasks with retrieval-augmented reasoning. Slower than Z1-32B but more thorough. BNB 4-bit for single-GPU.

models.name: Huihui Qwen3.5 27B Abliterated (Opus distill)
models.description: Qwen3.5-27B abliterated by huihui-ai using Claude Opus 4.6 reasoning distillation. BNB NF4 4-bit on single GPU. HuggingFace safetensors (55GB BF16, quantized on load).

models.name: Huihui Qwen3.5 4B Abliterated (Opus distill)
models.description: Qwen3.5-4B abliterated by huihui-ai using Claude Opus 4.6 reasoning distillation. Native BF16 safetensors (~9.3GB). Note: smaller variants in this series show more hallucinations than 35B.

models.name: Qwen3.5 35B-A3B Uncensored Q5_K_M
models.description: Qwen3.5 35B MoE (3B active params) abliterated by HauhauCS. Q5_K_M GGUF via llama.cpp — single RTX 3090. 262K native context. Hybrid DeltaNet+attention architecture. Best 35B quality-per-VRAM option.

models.name: Qwen3.5 35B-A3B Uncensored Q8_0
models.description: Qwen3.5 35B MoE (3B active params) abliterated by HauhauCS. Q8_0 GGUF via llama.cpp — dual RTX 3090 (35GB). Maximum quality, near-lossless quantization. 262K native context.

models.name: Qwopus MoE 35B-A3B Q4_K_M
models.description: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B MoE (3B active params) fine-tuned with Opus reasoning distillation via QLoRA by samuelcardillo. Q4_K_M GGUF (~21GB) fits single RTX 3090. 128K native context. Same base architecture as HauhauCS uncensored variant.

models.name: Qwopus MoE 35B-A3B Q5_K_M
models.description: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B MoE (3B active params) fine-tuned with Opus reasoning distillation via QLoRA by samuelcardillo. Q5_K_M GGUF (~25GB) — dual RTX 3090 (just over single 24GB limit). Better quality than Q4_K_M.

models.name: Qwopus MoE 35B-A3B Q8_0
models.description: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B MoE (3B active params) fine-tuned with Opus reasoning distillation via QLoRA by samuelcardillo. Q8_0 GGUF (~35GB) — dual RTX 3090 (48GB total). Model weights use ~43.8GB VRAM leaving ~2.7GB for KV cache; 98K context uses ~1.9GB KV cache (safe). Near-lossless quality.

models.name: BGE Base EN v1.5
models.description: Text embedding model for semantic search and RAG. Always-on for vector operations.
